title: Indulgent Molten Polish Shrimp Paste Dumplings
description: A floral, molten dumpling that blends the savory depth of shrimp paste with the lightness of steamed shrimp-kapi, ideal for a low-fat Polish-inspired indulgence.
instructions: In a bowl whisk flour, baking powder, and cornstarch until combined.
Whisk together soy sauce, rice vinegar, sesame oil, grated ginger, and garlic to make the umami base.
Stir in shrimp paste and egg white into the liquid mixture until smooth.
Pour the wet mixture into the dry ingredients and mix until a sticky dough forms.
Fold in chopped shrimp and shredded cabbage, ensuring they are evenly distributed.
Divide dough into 12 equal portions and roll each into a small ball, about 2.5 cm in diameter.
Place the dumpling balls onto parchment‑lined steamer trays, ensuring they do not touch.
Steam the dumplings over medium heat for 12 minutes until the dough is translucent and the shrimp is cooked.
Prepare a garnish of sliced green onions, lemon zest, and a drizzle of low-fat yogurt while steaming.
Serve the molten dumplings hot, garnished, with a floral soy dipping sauce.
